name: Zamariain Viewpoint Trail
name_original: Mirador de Zamariain – trail run desde Garaioa (SL-NA 58 A)
description: This difficult 4.2-mile trail run from Garaioa leads to the Mirador de Zamariain, offering panoramic views of the Irati Valley.
region_intro: Jogging routes Garaioa traverse a landscape characterized by the extensive Irati Forest, one of Europe's largest beech and oak woodlands. The region is situated within the Navarrese Pyrenees, featuring foothills and valleys carved by rivers like the Zorrate and Irati. This diverse topography offers a mix of forest trails, riverside paths, and routes with varying elevations. Best jogging routes around Garaioa The most popular jogging route is Irabia Reservoir - Irati River loop from Presa de Irabia, an 8.9 miles (14.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 48 minutes to complete. This route offers a scenic loop around the Irabia reservoir and along the Irati River. Another top favourite among local runners is Zamariain Viewpoint Trail, a difficult 4.2 miles (6.7 km) path. This route provides panoramic vistas of the Irati Aritza oak forest. Local runners also love the The Paradises - Erlan Trail (SL-NA 54C), a 3.0 miles (4.8 km) trail leading through the Irati Forest with views of the Irabia reservoir, often completed in about 34 minutes. Jogging around Garaioa is defined by extensive beech and oak forests, Pyrenean foothills, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy forest paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Garaioa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.25 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore Garaioa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running trails are available around Garaioa?
A: There are over 15 running routes around Garaioa, offering a diverse range of experiences. The komoot community has explored 17 different trails in the area, ensuring plenty of options for your next run.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging routes near Garaioa?
A: Jogging routes in Garaioa primarily traverse the extensive Irati Forest, one of Europe's largest beech and oak woodlands. You'll find a mix of forest paths, riverside trails along the Zorrate and Irati Rivers, and routes with varying elevations as you explore the foothills of the Navarrese Pyrenees.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Garaioa?
A: The region experiences mild summers, making it an ideal time for outdoor activities like running. Autumn is also spectacular, as the Irati Forest transforms into a vibrant display of reds and ochres, offering a breathtaking backdrop for your run.

Q: Are there options for different fitness levels?
A: Yes, Garaioa offers routes for various abilities. While most of the 17 available routes are moderate (10 routes), there is also an easy route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971491719/the-paradises-erlan-trail-sl-na-54c" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Paradises - Erlan Trail (SL-NA 54C)&lt;/a&gt;, and 6 more challenging trails for experienced runners.

Q: Are there any family-friendly jogging routes in Garaioa?
A: For an easier, family-friendly option, consider &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971491719/the-paradises-erlan-trail-sl-na-54c" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Paradises - Erlan Trail (SL-NA 54C)&lt;/a&gt;. This easy 3.0-mile route through the Irati Forest offers scenic views of the Irabia reservoir and is often completed in about 34 minutes, making it suitable for a leisurely family jog.

Q: Where can I find routes with scenic viewpoints?
A: The region boasts several stunning viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971389073/zamariain-viewpoint-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zamariain Viewpoint Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice, offering magnificent panoramic vistas of the Irati Aritza oak forest. Additionally, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3978369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tapla Pass&lt;/a&gt; also provides spectacular views and serves as an access point to the Irati Forest.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the running paths?
A: While primarily focused on natural beauty, you can find historical points of interest. The village of Garaioa itself features the 13th-century Church of San Andrés Apóstol. Nearby, the 18th-century Orbaizeta Royal Arms Factory is set in a spectacular natural location, and megalithic monuments can be found in the Olaberrialdea-Txotxarraldea area.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Garaioa?
A: The running routes in Garaioa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.25 stars from more than 7 reviews. Runners often praise the immersive experience within the Irati Forest and the diverse topography.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for experienced runners seeking a challenge, there are 6 difficult routes available. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971389073/zamariain-viewpoint-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zamariain Viewpoint Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 4.2-mile path with significant elevation gain, providing panoramic vistas of the Irati Aritza oak forest.

Q: Are there any routes that follow rivers or reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38157506" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Irabia Reservoir - Irati River loop from Presa de Irabia&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This 8.9-mile route offers a scenic loop around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2457928" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Irabia Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and continues along the Irati River, providing beautiful waterside views.

Q: What wildlife might I encounter while jogging in the Irati Forest?
A: The Irati Forest is a rich habitat for wildlife. While jogging, you might spot deer, squirrels, foxes, and wild boars. The forest's extensive beech and oak trees provide a natural sanctuary for these animals.

Q: Are there circular running routes available?
A: Many routes in the Garaioa area are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38157506" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Irabia Reservoir - Irati River loop from Presa de Irabia&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular route that allows you to start and finish at the same point.

Q: Are there any running routes that start from nearby towns or villages?
A: Yes, you can find routes originating from other nearby locations. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31813340" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Aurizberri&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.3-mile trail that starts from the village of Aurizberri, offering a different starting point for your exploration.
tips: Simply wonderful! A unique place with incredible panoramic views of the entire Irati River valley and beyond. It is a very picturesque and photogenic place in itself, just be careful to take the classic photo sitting on the top of the cliff, you have come all the way to enjoy the views, do not take unnecessary risks.

faqs.question: Is the Mirador de Zamariain trail run suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Komoot classifies this route as 'difficult' for jogging, requiring very good physical condition due to its 310 meters of elevation gain over 6.73 km. While some sources describe it as 'simple' for hikers, maintaining a continuous jog on the ascent can be demanding. If you're new to trail running, be prepared for a challenge, or consider a brisk walk.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Mirador de Zamariain trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers varied terrain. You'll start on cattle tracks through open fields before entering a forest. The path then transitions to forest paths, wider dirt tracks, and even some paved and cemented sections. Expect both open areas and pleasant, shaded stretches, especially during the climb.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route to Mirador de Zamariain?
faqs.answer: From the start, you'll enjoy picturesque meadows flanked by mountains. The route winds through beautiful oak and beech forests. The main highlight is the Zamariain Viewpoint, which offers breathtaking panoramic views of the Irati Valley, the Betelu oak forest, and surrounding peaks like Corona, Larrogain, Higa de Monreal, and Peña de Izaga.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Mirador de Zamariain trail?
faqs.answer: The trail officially starts from Garaioa. However, for a shorter walk or run, you have the option to drive part of the way and park approximately 1.3 km from the Zamariain Viewpoint itself.

faqs.question: Is the Mirador de Zamariain trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses cattle tracks and natural areas, making it generally suitable for dogs. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or potential wildlife, and to follow local regulations.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the Mirador de Zamariain trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant foliage, while summer provides lush greenery. Consider checking local weather forecasts, especially for trail running, as conditions can vary.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Mirador de Zamariain - trail run from Garaioa (SL-NA 58 A). It is a publicly accessible local trail.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I see on the trail?
faqs.answer: The route passes through forests and open areas, offering opportunities to spot local wildlife. Eagles have been reported in the area, so keep an eye on the skies. You might also encounter various birds and smaller forest animals.

faqs.question: Is the SL-NA 58 A trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The Mirador de Zamariain - trail run from Garaioa (SL-NA 58 A) is primarily an out-and-back route. The full trail from Garaioa to the viewpoint and back is approximately 6.6 to 7 kilometers.

faqs.question: How well-marked is the Mirador de Zamariain trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is well-signposted, making navigation straightforward. Look for the distinctive yellow, green, and white SL (local trail) markers along the route.

faqs.question: What makes the Mirador de Zamariain viewpoint so special?
faqs.answer: The Zamariain Viewpoint is renowned for its dramatic rock outcrop, which creates a thrilling sensation of being suspended over the abyss. It offers truly spectacular panoramic views and is a very popular spot for unique photographs, often described as one of Spain's most awe-inspiring viewpoints.
